title: How to Register for a Business Number (BN) in Canada

author.name: Elena Petrova
author.bio: An international business lawyer by trade, Elena breaks down the complexities of freelance contracts, corporate structures, and international liability. Her goal is to empower freelancers with the legal knowledge to operate confidently.

reviewedBy.name: Dr. Alistair Finch
reviewedBy.bio: With a Ph.D. in Economics and over 15 years at a Big Four accounting firm, Alistair specializes in demystifying cross-border tax law for independent professionals. He focuses on risk mitigation and long-term financial planning.

key_takeaways: Secure a Business Number to signal professional legitimacy to international clients and use it on W-8BEN-E forms to prevent US tax withholding.
Treat the $30,000 CAD worldwide revenue mark not as a limit to stay under, but as a non-negotiable trigger to proactively register for a GST/HST account.
Immediately update your invoices to display your full GST/HST number for Canadian clients, a legal requirement that also reinforces your status as a compliant business.
Recognize that a federal Business Number is not the final step; you must also complete your provincial business name registration to operate legally and open a business bank account.
summary: For independent professionals, being perceived as a "gig worker" by global clients creates payment friction and undermines credibility. The core advice is to proactively register for a Canadian Business Number (BN) as a strategic move to formalize your enterprise, rather than simply waiting to meet a compliance threshold. This action establishes you as a legitimate professional services provider, minimizing administrative hurdles, strengthening your negotiating position, and empowering you to operate with greater authority and control.
ai_overview_answer:
A Canadian Business Number (BN) is a strategic move for a solo business, becoming mandatory once your worldwide revenue from taxable supplies exceeds $30,000 CAD in four consecutive quarters. Proactively registering for a BN signals legitimacy to international clients, elevating your status from a gig worker to a professional services provider. For US expats in Canada, a BN is critical for cleanly separating business activities for tax reporting. The fastest way to obtain a BN is through the Canada Revenue Agency's (CRA) online portal, which often issues the number instantly.

Once registered, you must open a GST/HST program account (identified by "RT" after your BN) to legally charge and remit sales tax. This full GST/HST number must be displayed on invoices to Canadian clients. For US clients, the nine-digit BN serves as your Foreign Tax Identification Number on the W-8BEN-E form, preventing tax withholding. Finally, remember that obtaining a federal BN is separate from the legal requirement to register your business name with your province.

itemListElement.name: How to Register for a Business Number (BN) in Canada

@graph.headline: How to Register for a Business Number (BN) in Canada
@graph.description: Learn when and how to register for a business number in Canada. Our strategic guide covers the $30k threshold, online process, and invoicing global clients.

step.name: Your Preparation Checklist
step.text: Before starting the online registration, gather your personal identifiers (full name, SIN, date of birth), contact details, business structure and name, a description of your business activity, and your expected annual revenues.

step.name: The Recommended Path: The Business Registration Online (BRO) Portal
step.text: Use the Canada Revenue Agency's (CRA) Business Registration Online (BRO) portal for the fastest method. The process takes under 30 minutes and often issues your nine-digit Business Number instantly upon completion.

step.name: Understanding Program Accounts
step.text: Your nine-digit BN is a permanent identifier. You will attach specific program accounts to it, such as the GST/HST (RT) account, which is mandatory once you exceed $30,000 CAD in worldwide revenue, and the Payroll (RP) account, which is only needed if you incorporate and pay yourself a salary.

step.name: Fortify Your Invoices for Global Clients
step.text: Update your invoices to include your full GST/HST number for Canadian clients. Use your nine-digit BN as your Foreign Tax Identification Number on the W-8BEN-E form for US clients to prevent tax withholding. For EU clients, your registration simplifies VAT accounting through the 'reverse-charge mechanism'.

step.name: Establish Your Compliance Dashboard
step.text: Create a system to track the GST/HST you collect from Canadian clients versus the GST/HST you pay on business expenses (Input Tax Credits). You will remit the difference to the government at the end of your reporting period.

step.name: Fulfill Your Provincial Obligations
step.text: After securing your federal BN, you must also register your business name in your province. This is a separate legal requirement, especially if operating under a trade name, and is often necessary to open a business bank account.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between a business number and a GST/HST number?

acceptedAnswer.text: Think of the Business Number (BN) as your business's primary nine-digit identifier with the federal government (e.g., 123456789). A GST/HST number is not a separate number; it is your BN with the "RT" program account identifier attached (e.g., `123456789 RT 0001`). You must have a BN before you can open a GST/HST account.

mainEntity.name: Can a non-resident get a business number in Canada?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, a non-resident can and often must get a BN, but only if they are considered to be "carrying on business in Canada." The CRA determines this based on factors like where your services are delivered, where contracts are made, and where you solicit business. Simply having Canadian clients while operating entirely from abroad is often considered "carrying on business *with* Canada," not *in* Canada, and may not require registration. However, if your activities establish a significant presence, registration becomes mandatory.

mainEntity.name: Do I need a business number to freelance for a US client from Canada?

acceptedAnswer.text: While you are only required to register for a BN and GST/HST account once your worldwide revenue exceeds $30,000 CAD, doing so proactively is a powerful strategic move. It signals to US corporate clients that you are a legitimate business entity, not a gig worker. This builds trust, reduces friction in their payment systems, and strengthens your position as a credible international partner.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to get a business number in Canada?

acceptedAnswer.text: The speed depends on your registration method. Using the CRA's Business Registration Online (BRO) service or calling the business enquiries line is fastest, often providing your BN instantly. Applying by mail or fax using Form RC1 can take several weeks.

body.content: Achieving complete control over your global operation begins by reframing a critical question: not *if* you need a Canadian Business Number (BN), but *when* it becomes a strategic imperative. For the elite professional, this is not about rote compliance; it's about proactively structuring your business to command respect, minimize friction, and maintain absolute authority. Let's move past the bureaucracy and analyze this as the critical business decision it is.
The Strategic Imperative: From Gig Worker to Professional Services Provider
The decision to register for a BN is the first step in formalizing your solo enterprise, shifting both external perception and your internal approach to financial management.
The Signal of Legitimacy

To high-value corporate clients, especially those in the US and EU, a registered Business Number is a powerful signal of legitimacy and an immediate differentiator. Without it, you risk being categorized as a "gig worker"—a classification that can introduce payment friction, administrative hurdles, and a perception of impermanence. With a BN, you instantly elevate your status to a "professional services provider." This simple shift strengthens your negotiating position and builds the trust necessary for larger, long-term engagements. It is the foundation of a defensible, professional, and globally respected operation.
The $30,000 Threshold: A Compliance Trigger, Not a Goal

The Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) designates businesses with worldwide revenue from taxable supplies of $30,000 CAD or less in the last four consecutive calendar quarters as "small suppliers," who are not required to register for a Goods and Services Tax/Harmonized Sales Tax (GST/HST) account. For a high-earning professional, this isn't a revenue target to stay under; it's a compliance checkpoint you will likely surpass. Proactively registering for a BN and its corresponding GST/HST account once you cross this threshold is a non-negotiable step in building a resilient business. Waiting until you are forced to register creates unnecessary risk and signals a reactive, rather than strategic, approach to your finances.
A Framework for Your Decision
Your unique circumstances dictate the urgency of securing a BN. Consider which of these profiles best reflects your operation:

header: Strategic Importance of a Business Number
rows: A BN is critical for cleanly separating your Canadian business activities for the CRA. It creates a distinct financial identity for your Canadian operation, which is invaluable when managing complex US tax obligations. This formal separation simplifies reporting for mechanisms like the Foreign Bank and Financial Accounts Report (FBAR) and helps substantiate claims for the Foreign Earned Income Exclusion (FEIE) by clearly delineating your Canadian-earned income.

If you are a Canadian living abroad but still servicing clients in Canada, a BN and associated GST/HST number are essential. They ensure your invoices are compliant and professional, and more importantly, they demonstrate your continued economic ties to Canada. This can have significant, positive implications under various international tax treaties, preventing issues of double taxation and confirming your business's Canadian roots.

For a successful sole proprietor, a BN is the first official step in formalizing your business entity. It solidifies your operation in the eyes of the government and major clients. As your revenue grows, the BN becomes the foundation from which you can later decide to incorporate—a strategic move that can offer significant liability protection, greater credibility with investors, and tax advantages suited to a high-earning global professional.

body.content: Securing Your Business Number: A Precise Walkthrough
Once you've identified the strategic need, the execution is refreshingly direct. The process is a simple, one-time setup that grants you long-term control and peace of mind.
Your Preparation Checklist

Before opening the registration portal, preparing your information is the key to a swift process. The CRA's Business Registration Online (BRO) service is efficient, but sessions can time out. Have these details ready:

items: **Personal Identifiers:** Your full legal name, Social Insurance Number (SIN), and date of birth.
**Contact & Address Details:** Your personal postal code, a physical business address, and a mailing address (if different).
**Business Structure & Name:** Your business type (for most, this will be "sole proprietor") and the legal name of your business.
**Description of Business Activity:** A clear, concise description of your primary services (e.g., "management consulting services," "professional writing and editing," "software development").
**GST/HST Information:** Your expected total annual revenues to determine if you need to register for a GST/HST account.

body.content: The Recommended Path: The Business Registration Online (BRO) Portal

The fastest method is through the CRA's BRO portal. This online service is a straightforward, multi-step process that you can complete in under 30 minutes with the checklist above. Once you verify your identity, the portal guides you through entering your business details and selecting the program accounts you need. In most cases, your nine-digit Business Number is issued instantly upon completion.
Understanding Program Accounts

Your nine-digit BN is your permanent identifier for all dealings with the federal government. Its real functionality comes from the specific program accounts attached to it. Think of the BN as your main file folder and program accounts as the labeled dividers within it. For a "Business-of-One," two are paramount:

rows: This is your GST/HST account. You need this to legally charge and remit sales tax on your invoices to Canadian clients. It's mandatory once your worldwide revenue from taxable supplies exceeds $30,000 CAD in four consecutive quarters. Your full GST/HST number will appear as `123456789 RT 0001`.

This is a payroll account for remitting deductions like income tax and CPP contributions. As a sole proprietor, you do not need this account, as you pay yourself through owner's draws. This account only becomes necessary if you incorporate and pay yourself a formal salary.

body.content: Special Consideration: The Quebec Distinction
Operating in Quebec introduces a critical difference. Revenu Québec administers both the provincial QST and the federal GST/HST for businesses in the province.

items: If you operate in Quebec and *only* need a GST/HST account, you will register directly with Revenu Québec, not the CRA.

However, if you also need other federal program accounts (such as a payroll or import/export account), you must still obtain a federal BN from the CRA for those specific programs. Understanding this division of responsibility is essential.

body.content: The Activation Plan: Putting Your New Asset to Work

Receiving your Business Number isn't the finish line; it’s the starting pistol. You must now activate this asset to fortify your business, professionalize your operations, and eliminate compliance anxiety.
Module 1: Fortify Your Invoices for Global Clients
Your invoice is no longer just a request for payment; it is a declaration of your professional legitimacy. Upgrade it immediately.

items: **For Canadian Clients:** Your full GST/HST account number (`123456789 RT 0001`) must be clearly displayed. This signals to clients and the CRA that you are a registered, compliant business responsible for sales tax.

**For US Clients:** Use your nine-digit BN as your Foreign Tax Identification Number on the W-8BEN-E form. This form certifies your foreign status, preventing US clients from withholding 30% of your payment for tax purposes under the U.S.-Canada Tax Treaty.

**For EU Clients:** Your registered status simplifies transactions. For business-to-business services, the responsibility for VAT typically falls to the EU client through a "reverse-charge mechanism." Your invoice should note this applies, providing the proof your client needs to account for VAT correctly on their end.

body.content: Module 2: Establish Your Compliance Dashboard
Year-end tax stress is a choice. Eliminate it by creating a simple tracking system now. The core task is to monitor the GST/HST you collect versus the GST/HST you pay on business expenses.

items: **GST/HST Collected:** The tax portion of every payment from a Canadian client. This is money you are holding in trust for the government.
**GST/HST Paid (Input Tax Credits):** The tax you pay on legitimate business expenses, such as software or professional development. These are Input Tax Credits (ITCs) that you can claim back.

body.content: At the end of your reporting period, you remit the difference between what you collected and what you paid. A simple spreadsheet or accounting software transforms this from a chaotic scramble into a simple reporting task.
Module 3: Fulfill Your Provincial Obligations
Your federal BN from the CRA is a monumental step, but it is not the final one. In most provinces, you must also register your business name.

items: **Federal BN:** Identifies your business for tax purposes with the Government of Canada.

**Provincial Registration:** Legally establishes your right to use your business name within a specific province. For a sole proprietor operating under a trade name (e.g., "Apex Consulting" instead of "Jane Doe"), this is a legal requirement and is often necessary to open a business bank account.

body.content: Frequently Asked Questions
For a global professional, clarity is control. Here are answers to the most common points of confusion.
**What is the difference between a business number and a GST/HST number?**

Think of the Business Number (BN) as your business's primary nine-digit identifier with the federal government (e.g., 123456789). A GST/HST number is not a separate number; it is your BN with the "RT" program account identifier attached (e.g., `123456789 RT 0001`). You must have a BN before you can open a GST/HST account.
**Can a non-resident get a business number in Canada?**

Yes, a non-resident can and often must get a BN, but only if they are considered to be "carrying on business in Canada." The CRA determines this based on factors like where your services are delivered, where contracts are made, and where you solicit business. Simply having Canadian clients while operating entirely from abroad is often considered "carrying on business *with* Canada," not *in* Canada, and may not require registration. However, if your activities establish a significant presence, registration becomes mandatory.
**Do I need a business number to freelance for a US client from Canada?**

While you are only required to register for a BN and GST/HST account once your worldwide revenue exceeds $30,000 CAD, doing so proactively is a powerful strategic move. It signals to US corporate clients that you are a legitimate business entity, not a gig worker. This builds trust, reduces friction in their payment systems, and strengthens your position as a credible international partner.
**How long does it take to get a business number in Canada?**

The speed depends on your registration method. Using the CRA's Business Registration Online (BRO) service or calling the business enquiries line is fastest, often providing your BN instantly. Applying by mail or fax using Form RC1 can take several weeks.
Conclusion: From Operator to CEO

The arrival of your Business Number marks a fundamental shift in your professional identity. This is not about fulfilling a bureaucratic requirement; it is about graduating from a freelance operator who reacts to compliance demands into the CEO of a global enterprise.
Think of your Business Number as both a shield and a signal.

items: **As a Shield:** It defends you against compliance ambiguity and financial risk. With a BN and its associated tax accounts, you create a defensible structure for your operations, dissolving the anxiety that plagues so many independent professionals.

**As a Signal:** To your international clients, it communicates credibility. It confirms you are a serious, professional services provider, smoothing the onboarding process and validating your position as a reliable global partner.

body.content: By securing this asset, you are seizing control. You are architecting a business, not just completing projects. The administrative tasks are no longer sources of stress but components of a well-oiled machine that you command. You are no longer just a freelancer navigating a complex system; you are the CEO, fully empowered to focus on the high-value work that truly matters.
